modules.name: Catalog
modules.responsibility: Manages catalog items, brands, and types. Owns product data including name, description, price, picture URI, brand and type associations. Exposes read/write operations for catalog management.

evidence.reason: Core catalog entity with rich domain methods (UpdateDetails, UpdateBrand, UpdateType, UpdatePictureUri)
REST endpoint mapped to GET api/catalog-brands — confirms external API surface
Blazor service calls api/catalog-items endpoints for full CRUD — tight coupling to PublicApi

open_questions: Complete set of CatalogItem API endpoints (Create, Edit, Delete, GetById) not fully visible in Layer 1 source artifacts — PublicApi/CatalogItemEndpoints/ folder was not present in provided source files.
Is catalog image storage managed locally or via Azure Blob? PictureUri uses a template replacement pattern suggesting external URL.

modules.name: Basket
modules.responsibility: Manages shopping basket lifecycle: creation, item addition, quantity updates, anonymous-to-authenticated transfer, deletion, and checkout guard. Owns Basket and BasketItem aggregates.
entry_points: IBasketService.AddItemToBasket (called by Web/API controllers — not visible in Layer 1)

evidence.reason: Aggregate root with clear encapsulation — private _items list, public read-only Items, domain methods AddItem/RemoveEmptyItems
Application service implementing IBasketService — all basket operations go through this service, uses IRepository<Basket>
Query-optimized service for basket item count — direct EF Core usage to avoid in-memory sum

open_questions: Where are basket-related API/Razor page controllers in the Web project? Not present in Layer 1 source artifacts.
Anonymous basket transfer flow — is it triggered by cookie/session middleware or explicit controller action?

modules.name: Order
modules.responsibility: Manages order creation from basket checkout. Owns Order, OrderItem, Address, and CatalogItemOrdered aggregates. Converts basket items to immutable order items with price snapshot.
entry_points: IOrderService.CreateOrderAsync (called by Web checkout — not visible in Layer 1)

evidence.reason: DDD aggregate root — private _orderItems list, encapsulated Total() calculation, guard validations in constructor
CreateOrderAsync reads basket, fetches catalog items, creates CatalogItemOrdered snapshots, then saves Order — full checkout orchestration
Snapshot value object — comment says 'If catalog item details change, details of the item that was part of a completed order should not change'

open_questions: Where is the checkout controller/page in the Web project? Not in Layer 1 source artifacts.
Is there any order status management (fulfilled, cancelled) beyond creation? Not visible in available artifacts.
Is email notification sent on order creation? IEmailSender is defined but EmailSender is a stub (Task.CompletedTask).

modules.name: Identity
modules.responsibility: Manages authentication and authorization. Owns ASP.NET Identity integration, JWT token generation, user and role management, and identity database context.
entry_points: GET User (BlazorAdmin CustomAuthStateProvider fetches user info)

evidence.reason: JWT token creation using hardcoded key from AuthorizationConstants.JWT_SECRET_KEY — security risk
Hardcoded JWT_SECRET_KEY and DEFAULT_PASSWORD with TODO comments — known risk

open_questions: Does the Web project use cookie-based authentication separately from the JWT used by PublicApi? Both IdentityConnection and CatalogConnection exist.
Is the BlazorAdmin authentication flow via token from PublicApi only, or does it also use cookie auth from Web?

modules.name: Admin
modules.responsibility: Provides administrative interface for catalog management via Blazor WebAssembly UI. Allows CRUD operations on catalog items through PublicApi. Manages local browser cache for catalog data.

evidence.reason: Blazor component loading catalog items, types, brands via ICatalogItemService and ICatalogLookupDataService
DI registration of all BlazorAdmin services — all catalog-focused, no basket/order UI
baseUrls.apiBase = PublicApi URL — confirms dependency on PublicApi for data

open_questions: Are there Blazor admin pages for orders or users? Only CatalogItemPage is visible in Layer 1.
Is BlazorAdmin embedded within the Web project or deployed independently?

modules.name: CrossCutting
modules.responsibility: Provides shared infrastructure concerns: logging abstraction (IAppLogger/LoggerAdapter), JSON serialization extensions, guard clause extensions, shared exception types, URI composition, and email sender stub.

evidence.reason: Generic EfRepository<T> implements IRepository<T> and IReadRepository<T> using Ardalis.Specification — used by all domain modules
Comment says 'eliminates need to depend directly on ASP.NET Core logging types' — explicit decoupling intent
Stub implementation — Task.CompletedTask only with TODO comment — unimplemented concern

open_questions: EmailSender is a stub (returns Task.CompletedTask). Is email notification planned? What triggers it?
Is there any caching infrastructure (Redis, memory cache) beyond the BlazorAdmin local storage cache?
